# Direct UX And Privacy Checklist: Direct Zed Translation

**Purpose**: Validate direct-action UX, platform-boundary, privacy, and non-mutation requirements quality before task generation
**Created**: 2026-07-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the direct-action requirements complete for discovery, invocation, result access, and independence from Agent configuration? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Contract §Entry Point]
- [x] CHK002 Are input requirements complete for non-empty selection and no-selection open-document flows? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002-FR-003, Contract §Target Selection]
- [x] CHK003 Are read-only preview requirements defined for successful output, invalidation, and source separation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004-FR-005, Contract §Preview Contract]
- [x] CHK004 Are copy/insert/apply boundaries explicit enough to prevent unsupported or constitution-violating scope from entering tasks? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005-FR-006, Research §Decision 3]
- [x] CHK005 Are provider requirements complete for offline, local, remote, confirmation, secret blocking, and payload minimization? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008-FR-012]
- [x] CHK006 Are safe failure requirements complete across validation, protocol, provider, timeout, platform, notification, and logs? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013-FR-014, LSP Contract §Error Mapping]
- [x] CHK007 Are TDD and manual Zed evidence requirements stated before behavioral task generation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015-FR-TEST-A, Quickstart]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK008 Is "action owned by the extension" resolved to a concrete supported Zed surface without redefining Agent/MCP as direct UX? [Clarity, Plan §Summary, Research §Decision 1]
- [x] CHK009 Is the distinction between selection and open-document fallback objective and based on an empty/non-empty range? [Clarity, Contract §Target Selection]
- [x] CHK010 Is "reliable selection" defined through document URI, exact version, checked UTF-16 range, and protected-content classification? [Clarity, Plan §Constraints, Data Model §Translation Target]
- [x] CHK011 Is "readable preview" defined as full validated Markdown hover content rather than a short notification or diagnostic? [Clarity, Research §Decision 2, LSP Contract §Hover]
- [x] CHK012 Is the remote confirmation scope unambiguously per invocation rather than per provider, server, session, or document? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010, Direct Workflow §Remote Confirmation]
- [x] CHK013 Are safe provider-locality labels distinguished from forbidden provider URLs, paths, or credential details? [Clarity, Data Model §Provider Privacy State, Direct Workflow §Remote Confirmation]
- [x] CHK014 Are all inherited input, segment, output, and timeout limits stated with exact values? [Clarity, Plan §Performance Goals]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK015 Do spec, plan, contracts, and constitution consistently prohibit buffer/source edits even though LSP can represent workspace edits? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Plan §Constraints, LSP Contract §CodeAction]
- [x] CHK016 Do the spec and design consistently keep mock/offline as default and `translator-core` as the final privacy authority? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008-FR-012, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK017 Does the direct LSP path coexist with MCP compatibility without making MCP or Agent part of the primary workflow? [Consistency, Plan §Structure Decision, Zed Launch Contract]
- [x] CHK018 Are selection snapshot, command payload, and preview cache requirements consistent about URI/version/range identity and absence of source text in arguments? [Consistency, Data Model, LSP Contract]
- [x] CHK019 Are provider settings requirements consistent between existing wrapper allowlists and the new LSP launch contract? [Consistency, Zed Launch Contract, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK020 Does the no-persistence design align with log redaction, preview invalidation, and manual evidence requirements? [Consistency, Plan §Storage, Data Model §Translation Preview, Quickstart §Manual Evidence]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK021 Can the direct-workflow action-count criterion be measured without relying on Agent or implementation-specific internal calls? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [x] CHK022 Are non-mutation, Markdown preservation, unsafe-input denial, remote denial, payload minimization, and redaction outcomes expressed as 100% criteria? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002-SC-007]
- [x] CHK023 Does manual success require separate selection, open-document, and denied privacy/safety evidence? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-008]
- [x] CHK024 Is unavailable-platform-capability handling measurable and explicitly barred from undocumented Agent fallback? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-009, Spec §FR-014]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK025 Are primary selection scenarios covered for success, preview access, and unchanged source? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK026 Are alternate open-document scenarios covered for no selection, Markdown preservation, and workspace/file validation? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2]
- [x] CHK027 Are offline/local/remote provider variants and confirmation denial covered in the direct workflow? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3]
- [x] CHK028 Are waiting, success-notification, hover-preview, safe-failure, and stale-preview states represented in the requirements and contracts? [Coverage, Data Model §State transitions, Direct Workflow §Successful Flow]
- [x] CHK029 Is keyboard invocation documented through Zed's standard code-action command so no pointer-only interaction is required? [Accessibility, Direct Workflow §Entry Point, Quickstart]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK030 Are blank, whitespace-only, invalid Unicode range, stale version, concurrent edit, closed document, and unsupported language cases addressed? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, LSP Contract]
- [x] CHK031 Are Markdown selections intersecting fences, inline code, links, HTML, frontmatter, or partial protected regions required to fail closed or preserve content rather than guess? [Edge Case, Plan §Constraints, Data Model §Translation Target]
- [x] CHK032 Are unsaved/unauthorized URI, traversal, symlink escape, sensitive filename, non-UTF-8, binary, and size boundary cases retained for the document fallback? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-SEC-B]
- [x] CHK033 Are multiple selections explicitly excluded instead of being merged or partially processed ambiguously? [Edge Case, Direct Workflow §Target Selection]
- [x] CHK034 Are remote confirmation dismissal, mismatched response, configured-but-not-allowlisted target, secret detection, timeout, malformed response, and oversized output covered? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Direct Workflow §Remote Confirmation]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK035 Is the dependency on Zed LSP code actions, execute-command, show-message request, and Markdown hover documented with current primary-source evidence? [Dependency, Research §Decisions 1-2]
- [x] CHK036 Are custom pane, clipboard, custom action API, Agent fallback, code-file support, publication, and Zed-core changes explicitly out of scope? [Scope, Plan §Scale/Scope, Research §Decisions 1-3]
- [x] CHK037 Are new Rust dependencies project-scoped, version-bounded, lockfile-controlled, and covered by the existing container workflow? [Dependency, Plan §Primary Dependencies, Constitution V]
- [x] CHK038 Is manual validation separated from automated protocol contracts without requiring real secrets or proprietary content? [Assumption, Quickstart §Manual Scenario 3, Quickstart §Manual Evidence]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: direct Zed UX, platform capability boundary, privacy/consent, stale-state safety, and non-mutation.
- Depth level: Standard reviewer checklist before `/speckit-tasks`.
- Actor/timing: Spec/PR reviewer before task generation.
- Result: 38/38 requirements-quality checks pass; no unresolved checklist gap blocks tasks.
